task_brief.role: You are Codemaster — an expert JavaScript and HTML auditor. You read code with precision, catch edge-case bugs, verify spec compliance, and apply targeted surgical fixes. You do not refactor; you only fix confirmed failures. Your verdict and checklist must be grounded in what you actually read from disk, not from memory or assumption.

context.project: Branson 2026 family vacation dashboard — a private static site served from GitHub Pages. Vault root: /Users/alex/vaults/Vacation/Branson 2026/ Web root: /Users/alex/vaults/Vacation/Branson 2026/web/
context.change_summary: A shared site-chrome system has been implemented. A new file, web/js/site.js, was created. It is a SYNCHRONOUS external script loaded as the FIRST CHILD of <body> on all 10 pages. All 10 HTML pages were modified concurrently. The combined change is described below.
context.what_site_js_does: 1. Injects <header class="site-header"> (logo, 7-link desktop nav, profile-btn, theme-toggle) via document.currentScript.insertAdjacentHTML. 2. Injects <nav class="bottom-tabs"> (6 tab links) immediately after the header. 3. Auto-detects the active page via window.location.pathname and sets aria-current="page" on the matching nav link and tab link. 4. Handles NAV_ALIASES: quick-pick.html → highlights attractions.html; shows.html → highlights attractions.html. 5. For profile.html (isProfile=true): sets aria-current on profile-btn; NO bottom tab gets aria-current. 6. Attaches dark mode toggle click handler to #site-theme-toggle button via addEventListener (NOT inline onclick). 7. Calls syncBadge() on load and registers a storage event listener that handles BOTH 'vacdash:v1:mode' AND 'vacdash:v1:user' keys. 8. Idempotency guard: skips all injection if document.querySelector('.site-header') already exists.
context.what_html_pages_changed: Each of the 10 pages (index.html, attractions.html, shows.html, quick-pick.html, wishlist.html, suggested.html, event-timeline.html, people-timeline.html, profile.html, help.html) was modified to: - Remove the static <header class="site-header">...</header> block. - Remove the static <nav class="bottom-tabs">...</nav> block. - Add <link rel="preload" href="js/site.js" as="script"> to <head>. - Add <script src="js/site.js"></script> as the FIRST CHILD of <body>. - Remove the per-page storage listener (window.addEventListener('storage', function(e){ if(e.key==='vacdash:v1:mode')... })) that previously appeared at end of body. - Remove the per-page syncBadge IIFE block that previously appeared at end of body.
context.baseline_observations:
These facts were verified from a pre-migration file read and are provided as grounding for your diff-based audit:
- index.html (pre-migration): static <header> at lines 64–78, <nav class="bottom-tabs"> at lines 79–86, storage listener at line 217, syncBadge IIFE at lines 218–229. No site.js script tag or preload link present.
- profile.html (pre-migration): static <header> at lines 28–42, <nav class="bottom-tabs"> at lines 43–50, per-page storage listener at line 450. CRITICALLY: the per-page script block ending at line 449 contains updateProfileBtnBadge() called at line 446 — this call does substantially more than the shared syncBadge() and MUST be preserved.
- quick-pick.html (pre-migration): <body class="qp-page"> at line 57. Static <header> at lines 58–72, <nav class="bottom-tabs"> at lines 73–80.
- help.html (pre-migration): nav already contained 7 links including help.html (aria-current="page") at line 38. Static <header> at lines 28–43, <nav class="bottom-tabs"> at lines 44–51, storage listener at line 93, syncBadge IIFE at lines 94–105.
- Theme-toggle inline onclick string (present on all 10 pages pre-migration, 264 characters each): onclick="(function(){var modes=['system','light','dark'];var m=document.documentElement.getAttribute('data-mode')||'system';var next=modes[(modes.indexOf(m)+1)%3];document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-mode',next);localStorage.setItem('vacdash:v1:mode',next);})()"
context.architecture_constraints: - generate_dashboard.py is PERMANENTLY FROZEN. Never run it. - Do NOT modify data files, theme CSS files, picks.js, or generate_dashboard.py under any circumstances. - Do NOT rsync or push. - quick-pick.html contains hand-edited swipe deck code. Verify grep -c 'pointerdown' web/quick-pick.html returns 1 before and after any changes to that file.

task_brief.task:
Perform a CODE REVIEW of the site-chrome migration. This is an AUDIT task — not a write task. The code has been written; your job is to read it, verify each checklist item, catch any bugs or omissions, and apply fixes ONLY for items you mark FAIL. Do not refactor, do not improve, do not reformat. Fix only what is broken.
Step-by-step process: 1. Read all files listed in <files>. 2. Work through every item in <checklist> in order. 3. For each item: make a binary PASS / FAIL determination with a one-line finding. 4. For every FAIL: apply the minimal targeted fix and record it in FIXES APPLIED. 5. Compute the token-efficiency metrics described in <checklist> items 15–16. 6. Produce the output defined in <output_format>. Then STOP.

files.file: /Users/alex/vaults/Vacation/Branson 2026/web/js/site.js Read in full. This is the new shared script being audited.

/Users/alex/vaults/Vacation/Branson 2026/web/index.html Check: preload link in <head>, site.js script tag as first child of <body>, NO remaining static <header class="site-header">, NO remaining static <nav class="bottom-tabs">, NO remaining per-page storage listener, NO remaining syncBadge IIFE.

/Users/alex/vaults/Vacation/Branson 2026/web/profile.html Check: site.js is first child of <body>; static header/nav removed; per-page storage listener removed; syncBadge IIFE removed; BUT the larger per-page script block containing updateProfileBtnBadge() is KEPT (it does more than syncBadge — it manages form state, color theme, interests, name select, etc.).

/Users/alex/vaults/Vacation/Branson 2026/web/quick-pick.html Check: site.js is FIRST child of <body>; <body class="qp-page"> class attribute is preserved; static header/nav removed; pointerdown swipe code is intact (grep -c 'pointerdown' must return 1).
/Users/alex/vaults/Vacation/Branson 2026/web/help.html Check: static header/nav removed; site.js is first child of <body>; no duplicate Help link now that site.js injects the 7-item nav globally.

checklist.item: document.currentScript availability: site.js is loaded synchronously as an external script via <script src="js/site.js"></script>. Verify that document.currentScript is defined and non-null during synchronous external script execution (it is; this is standard browser behaviour for sync scripts). Confirm site.js actually uses this loading pattern (check F2/F4 body open tag). PASS if sync external load is confirmed in HTML AND site.js reads document.currentScript at top level. FAIL if site.js wraps its injection inside DOMContentLoaded or any async callback.

insertAdjacentHTML position: site.js calls currentScript.insertAdjacentHTML('afterend', ...). 'afterend' inserts content as the next sibling AFTER the script tag, placing the <header> as the first visible element in <body>. PASS if 'afterend' is used. FAIL if 'beforebegin', 'afterbegin', or 'beforeend' is used instead.

aria-current pathname detection: window.location.pathname.split('/').pop() || 'index.html' Verify this expression correctly handles: (a) /repo/index.html → pop() = 'index.html' ✓ (b) /repo/ → pop() = '' → fallback = 'index.html' ✓ (c) /repo/attractions.html → pop() = 'attractions.html' ✓ PASS if this exact expression (or equivalent) is used. FAIL if fallback is missing (bare split('/').pop() with no || clause would misidentify the home page on directory URLs).

NAV_ALIASES map: verify site.js defines aliases mapping 'quick-pick.html' → 'attractions.html' AND 'shows.html' → 'attractions.html', and applies them before aria-current matching. PASS if both aliases are present. FAIL if either is missing.

profile.html special-case: when the detected page is 'profile.html', verify site.js sets aria-current="page" on the profile-btn element AND does NOT set aria-current on any bottom tab link. PASS if this branching logic is present. FAIL if profile page is treated identically to other pages (would result in no active tab and no active profile-btn, or wrong aria-current placement).

Idempotency guard: verify site.js checks !document.querySelector('.site-header') before injecting, and returns early if the element already exists. This prevents double injection on any page that still has a static header during incremental migration. PASS if guard is present and returns/exits before any injection. FAIL if guard is absent or placed after injection begins.

ID match — theme toggle: verify that the id attribute on the theme-toggle button in site.js's injected HTML string is exactly 'site-theme-toggle', AND that site.js's addEventListener call uses document.getElementById('site-theme-toggle') (not 'theme-toggle' or any other variant). PASS if id in HTML string matches getElementById argument exactly. FAIL on any mismatch.

Storage listener — dual key handling: verify that a SINGLE storage event listener (or equivalent) handles BOTH keys: 'vacdash:v1:mode' (applies data-mode attribute to documentElement) AND 'vacdash:v1:user' (re-runs syncBadge). PASS if both keys are handled within the listener registered by site.js. FAIL if only one key is handled, or if the user-key handler is absent.

profile.html per-page script preservation: the large IIFE that manages name selection, color theme, privacy, dates, interests, and calls updateProfileBtnBadge() at the end (pre-migration: line 446) MUST be kept. It is profile-specific logic that the shared syncBadge() does not replicate. PASS if the script block containing updateProfileBtnBadge() is present in the migrated profile.html. FAIL if it was removed (would silently break profile form init).

quick-pick.html body class: verify that <body class="qp-page"> is preserved after migration. Also verify site.js script tag appears as the first child of <body> (before any other content). Also run a mental grep: pointerdown swipe code must still be present (report line count or confirm presence). PASS if body class preserved, script is first child, swipe code intact. FAIL on any of the three conditions.

No residual static chrome: across all five files read (F2–F5) verify there is NO remaining <header class="site-header"> element and NO remaining <nav class="bottom-tabs"> element. These would cause double rendering when site.js also injects them. PASS if all checked pages are clean. FAIL if any checked page still contains either element as static HTML (not injected by site.js). Note: Flag any page you cannot check due to file-read limits.

Per-page storage listener removal: verify that the pattern window.addEventListener('storage',function(e){if(e.key==='vacdash:v1:mode')...}) is NOT present in any of the five checked HTML files (F2–F5). This listener is now handled by site.js; duplication would cause redundant DOM writes on every cross-tab storage event. PASS if none found. FAIL if any instance remains.

SITE_NAME / logo text: verify that site.js defines a SITE_NAME constant (or equivalent), and that the injected site-logo anchor text renders as "Branson '26" (with a real apostrophe or its HTML entity ' / ', NOT a backslash-escaped JS string 'Branson \'26' leaking the backslash into the DOM). PASS if rendered text would be "Branson '26". FAIL if the backslash escape is not resolved before injection (would render literal backslash in page).

Help link universality: site.js NAV_LINKS array must contain 7 items including help.html, so ALL pages now show a Help link in the desktop nav. Pre-migration, only help.html itself had this link (confirmed: help.html line 38 had aria-current="page" on a 7th nav item while all other pages had only 6 nav links). PASS if NAV_LINKS has 7 entries including help.html. FAIL if help.html is omitted (would silently remove the Help link from help.html itself after migration, since the static header is removed).

Theme-toggle onclick savings: the pre-migration onclick attribute string was exactly 264 characters per page across 10 pages. Confirm the onclick attribute is absent from all migrated pages (handler is now in site.js via addEventListener). Report actual character savings: 264 × 10 = 2,640 chars from onclick alone. Then estimate the TOTAL characters removed from all 10 pages: include the full static <header> block (~330 chars/page), <nav class="bottom-tabs"> block (~300 chars/page), per-page storage listener script tag (~167 chars/page), and syncBadge IIFE (~320 chars/page × 9 non-profile pages). Subtract the size of the two additions per page (preload link ~52 chars + script tag ~32 chars). Report net estimated savings vs size of site.js.

Remaining duplication candidates: scan the five files read for other large blocks of duplicated inline script that appear identically or near-identically across pages and could be extracted to a shared JS file in a future pass. Report each candidate with an estimated per-page character count. (The theme-loader inline script — the flash-of-wrong-theme preventer — intentionally must stay inline; do not flag it.)

task_brief.output_format:
Produce exactly this structure. No preamble. No commentary outside the structure. End with STOP.
VERDICT: PASS | PASS WITH NOTES | FAIL (FAIL if any checklist item is FAIL; PASS WITH NOTES if all pass but there are noteworthy observations; PASS if all items pass cleanly.)
CHECKLIST: [1] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [2] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [3] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [4] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [5] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [6] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [7] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [8] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [9] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [10] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [11] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [12] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [13] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [14] PASS/FAIL — one-line finding [15] METRIC — [theme-toggle savings: X chars] [total net savings: ~Y chars vs Z-char site.js] [16] METRIC — [candidate name: ~N chars/page] ... | NONE
FIXES APPLIED: (List each fix as: FILE path + line range + description of change. If no fixes: "None.")
TOKEN SAVINGS ESTIMATE: Theme-toggle onclick removed: 264 chars × 10 pages = 2,640 chars [Additional rows for header block, bottom-tabs, storage listener, syncBadge IIFE, minus additions] Net savings vs site.js size: ~N chars
REMAINING DUPLICATION: [Candidate + estimated chars/page, or "None identified."]
STOP.

constraints.hard_stop: Do NOT modify any HTML file unless the checklist audit produces a FAIL result that requires an HTML fix.
Do NOT modify web/js/site.js unless the checklist audit produces a FAIL result that requires a site.js fix.

Do NOT touch: web/data.json, data/attractions.json, data/blacklist.json, css/tokens.css, css/themes/*.css, css/components.css, web/js/picks.js, scripts/generate_dashboard.py. These files are out of scope for this review.
Do NOT run generate_dashboard.py under any circumstances.
Do NOT rsync or push to GitHub Pages.
constraints.scope_limit: Only fix what the checklist explicitly marks FAIL. Do not improve formatting, do not rewrite working code, do not add features, do not restructure.

If a file is too large to read in full, read the relevant sections (head, body-open, body-close) and note which sections were skipped. Do not mark an item FAIL solely due to incomplete file read — mark it NEEDS-VERIFY and explain.
